Economics is the study of how society is managing its scarce resources. In most societies, resources are allocated not by a single central planner but through the combined actions of millions of households and firms. Economists are studying how people are making decisions, how they are working, what they are buying, how much they are saving, and how they are investing their savings. Economists are also studying how people are interacting with one another. For example, they are examining how the multitude of buyers and sellers of a good are interacting to determine the price the good is sold at and the quantity is sold. Economists are studying forces and trends that are affecting the economy as a whole, including the growth of average income, the fraction of the population that can't find no work, and the rate prices are rising.

The fundamental lessons about individual decision making are that people are facing trade-offs, the cost of something is what you give up to get the thing, rational people are thinking at the margin, and people are responding to incentives. When people are making decisions, they must consider both the obvious and implicit costs of their actions. Economists are using the term opportunity cost to describe what you give up to get something. Because people are facing trade-offs, making decisions is requiring comparing the costs and benefits of alternative courses of action. Rational people are systematically and purposefully doing the best they can to achieve their objectives, given the available opportunities. Economists are assuming that people are rational and are responding to incentives. One important insight of economics is that incentives are mattering. When the price of a good is rising, buyers are tending to buy less and sellers are tending to sell more.

Public policy is able to change the costs or benefits people are facing and, as a result, is altering their behavior. For example, when the government is imposing a tax on the sale of petrol, people may be driving smaller cars, riding bicycles, or using public transport. Economists are trying to understand how policies are affecting people's behavior. The study of economics is offering many insights into how the world is working.
